warnings: fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for pyridine (11 atoms: N1, C2–C6, H7–H11) were computed at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard', density-fit, PySCF RKS), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N±1 states used multiplicity 2 (doublet). Geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied pyridine.xyz (no re-optimization). Partial charges were computed via the Mulliken scheme in PySCF for the neutral, cation (+1, doublet), and anion (−1, doublet) species; Fukui indices were obtained by finite difference.

Headline result: the most electrophilic site (largest f⁺) is N (atom 1) with f⁺ = 0.48422572. The most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻) is C4 (atom 4) with f⁻ = 0.11826260. Charge conservation was satisfied for both f⁺ and f⁻ (each summing to 1.0000). Integrity status: trustworthy = true.

All three integrity checks passed (listed verbatim in warnings). No additional engine warnings were reported.
